scriptId: cs_v2_OVERRIDE_L40_Medium-term plans for the next year
historyId: h_v2_OVERRIDE_L40_Medium-term plans for the next year

memories.content: User asked to identify 3-5 strategic objectives for a 12-month horizon across product, marketing, and operations. The response provided four objectives with purposes and primary domains: 1) Strengthen product-market fit and user retention (Purpose: deliver priority features and improve onboarding/UX to increase engagement and reduce churn; Primary: Product with Marketing support for messaging). 2) Accelerate revenue growth and conversion (Purpose: scale demand generation

User proposed a yearly hiring plan with a reasonable budget to recruit two backend engineers, one frontend engineer, one product manager, and one designer; Assistant acknowledged that the plan provides a clear capacity baseline for the year and offered to either map hires to prioritized objectives with a quarter-by-quarter staffing/timeline or draft specific initiatives and milestones (OKRs) aligned with each hire.

- The data defines acceptance criteria and success targets for two features (A and B), including purpose summaries and sections on functional acceptance, instrumentation/analytics, non-functional & compliance, operational readiness, rollout plan & rollback gates, and target launch metrics. It also includes general acceptance & measurement rules applying to both features and a note offering to create a launch checklist template.

Feature A
- Purpose: priority feature for early retention and conversion; must be stable for M6 launch and support marketing amplification.
- Functional acceptance: complete core user stories; end-to-end production happy path with no manual steps; UI/UX per design sign-off; accessibility basics; feature flagging by cohort; beta with internal and
- The user asked which parts of Feature A can be scoped down to an MVP when

- The response provides a practical plan for contracting a third-party analytics vendor, covering contract length, onboarding timeline, integration priorities, success criteria, roles, risk protections, and quick wins.

Contract length and structure
- Start with a 6-month pilot agreement (limited scope, included implementation hours, lower commitment).
- Post-pilot: convert to a 12–24 month contract with quarterly business reviews if

- Purpose: Provide a repeatable, lightweight governance cadence to stop wasting time on losing bets and reallocate resources quickly when signals require a course change.

- Cadence: Quarterly checkpoints at the end of each quarter (formal review), plus fast-track reviews anytime a “trigger” threshold is hit. Prework 1–2 days to validate data, 1 full checkpoint meeting (
